import io
import string
import list

fn parse_line(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut cur = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(v)
      v
    when None
      0
    end
    if b == 45
      neg = true
    else if b >= 48 and b <= 57
      cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
      has = true
    else
      if has
        if neg
          result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
        else
          result = list.push(result, cur)
        end
        cur = 0
        has = false
        neg = false
      else
        neg = false
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
    else
      result = list.push(result, cur)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn dedupe_append(_ acc: List<Int>, _ xs: List<Int>) -> List<Int>
  mut result = acc
  for x in xs
    if not list.contains(result, x)
      result = list.push(result, x)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn sort_list(_ xs: List<Int>) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  for x in xs
    mut inserted = false
    mut new_result: List<Int> = []
    for y in result
      if not inserted and x < y
        new_result = list.push(new_result, x)
        inserted = true
      end
      new_result = list.push(new_result, y)
    end
    if not inserted
      new_result = list.push(new_result, x)
    end
    result = new_result
  end
  result
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums1 = parse_line(line1)
let nums2 = parse_line(line2)
let empty: List<Int> = []
let combined = dedupe_append(dedupe_append(empty, nums1), nums2)
let sorted = sort_list(combined)

mut out = ""
mut first = true
for x in sorted
  if first
    out = "#{x}"
    first = false
  else
    out = "#{out} #{x}"
  end
end
println(out)
